The cyst is essentially within the inferior pulmonary ligament and extends all the way up to the inferior pulmonary vein.
Cautery is used to dissect it away from the surrounding pulmonary parenchyma and the vein as seen here.
We attempt to keep the cyst intact in order to allow us to appreciate the edge of the cyst in relation to the boundary of the esophageal mucosa so that we do not inadvertently injure it.
Once the mass is excised, we then insufflate the esophagus with an endoscope in order to check the integrity of the mucosa.
After resection, the esophageal muscular layer is re-approximated over the mucosa to prevent an esophageal diverticulum.
